Hey Jordan Belfort here, time for your daily motivation slash skills training. I want to talk about a skill today and it's a really important one. It's one that's probably the most overlooked sales strategy when it comes to really grow your business and it's about asking for referrals in the right way. But let me tell you something, the biggest misconception out there among salespeople is that they have to wait for some arbitrary period of time to pass before they can ask for a referral and feel comfortable and feel justified. In other words, you sell someone a product, you have to wait for X number of weeks or months until you show them that you were right, that your product delivered on the promises that you did a great job as a salesperson, you made them feel comfortable the whole way through. You have to wait for something to happen before you could ask for referrals with a straight face. In other words, how do you sell someone something and as soon as you say, oh by the way, even though you don't really know yet if it does what I say it does, can you give me all your friends, can you give me the numbers right? Well, bottom line is this, that's bullshit. I'm going to tell you a story here, but I was selling mortgages. This is a perfect example. I was in the mortgage business, right? I had a company, I was part of someone and it was in the early 2000s, 2001, 2002, right when the Refi boom was in full force before it was like, deserted in 2007, right? So I had this three or four year window, I was selling mortgages and I started going over the phone, you know, cold calling people, closing them, right? Then training others to do the same. Then I had this idea about going actually out into the field and going door to door selling mortgages, right? No one had tried it before and the first street I knocked down, bam, I closed two people on the first street, made $15,000 under an hour, I said shit, I'm locked or something, right? Now here's the deal, I would go into someone's house, I would sit down with them, they could start off being hostile or neutral, right? Typically a little bit hostile, right? At the time I was done with it, with the sale and closing them, they'd be on the phone calling all their friends saying, oh my God, I just met the greatest emotion, you have to use this guy, he's the best, like they're saying he's the best, I hadn't even done anything yet, I hadn't even submitted their paperwork yet, all I had done was build out an application for them and made them feel great about it. And then I asked them who else they knew I could help. I did say can you give me some referrals and say I don't know who you think I could help, the same way I helped you, here's the ticket, you can start asking for referrals and get a massive amount of them the day, the moment you close the sale, there's nothing wrong, there's no law that says any amount of time has to pass by, you have to show them performance, people will go right on the button, put themselves out on a limb and start recommending you. Why? Well, here's the way it works. Beautiful, I'm right by the wall, no way I'd say it's awesome, right? Anyway, so here's the way it works, here's the psychology, just so you understand, when someone tips over, they go pass that tipping point and they say yeah, I'll do it, they say yes, they buy, right? Well, the reason they're ready to call their friends if you ask them the right way is because they're looking to justify their own decision, in their mind they say yes, these days totally believe it, you've convinced them that you are the right person for the job, you're the best product, the company will step on it, they want to reinforce that to themselves by telling other people, asking their friends, so all you have to do is very calmly, since the tone of utter sincerity and the sort of empathy, so anyone else you know that you can actually think I can help with my service the same way I helped you, simple as that, anyone else you know that I can help in the same way I helped you, not anyone else you know who I can give you some referrals and I can make money from them, use the power of language, is there anybody else that you know, any friends, any family and neighbors here that I can help in the same way that I helped you? I'd really appreciate it, it'd be awesome, that's how you ask for a referral, now, let's go to the next step, which is what's better, just to get a name or actually have them call, obviously the answer is to have them call, see here's what I want you to do, I don't want you just to get names and phone numbers, I want you to start asking these people to reach out to them to let them know you'll be calling at least, but even better still if they'll dial the phone right there then patch the phone, that's the best of all, and let me tell you something, people do it all the time, don't kid yourself, it is standard operating position, whenever I sell something, whenever I close, the next question I ask is, oh, great, you made a great decision, by the way, you know anybody else, any friends, any family, any neighbors here that can help the same way I helped you, I'd really appreciate it, it'd be so awesome, and they'll start saying, let me think of it, they start searching, next thing you know, they'll say, oh yeah, I know, if people call, they'll give them a call right now, it'd be so great, I'm in the area, they'll say sure, why not, they start calling right there on the spot, you get it, the best time to ask for a referral is right when you close the sale, and the certainty is fresh in their mind, remember I said certainty, wanes, enthusiasm, wanes, everything wanes, so when they say yes, in that moment, you're basking in the afterglobal closed sale, they're basking in the afterglobal closed sale, they say yes, they feel good about it, if you made your airtight logical case, this is one of the straight lines of solution, because you're working on both types of certainty, logical and emotional certainty, what happens is when they say yes, they feel good about it, it's like you just impulse them, they're like, what the f**k did I do, I don't really, if you create the airtight logical case, they can connect all the thoughts, remember what I said about that, I said when someone is feeling logical certainty, not only can they connect all the thoughts from beginning to end to explain this to themselves, they can also tell that to another person, they can explain it to a third party, which makes them comfortable to tell someone else about a product, that's why with the straight line system, you get so many referrals, you set yourself up to get a massive amount of referrals by creating an airtight logical case and then moving them emotionally, that sets the stage, and after you close the deal, you don't go home, wait a week, wait too much, make sure that they feel good about it, that you perform the product, don't do that, right then and there, on the spot, while they're feeling that certainty, you say, oh by the way, if you don't anybody else who I can help, the same way I helped you, I really appreciate it, so awesome, so awesome, I really appreciate it, and they'll say, oh well, let me think it, you might get lucky, and they'll be out there on the phone, giving you 405 referrals, oh you might get unlucky, and they might not know anybody, but the point is, you have to ask everyone, it's a numbers game, I say yes, yes, yes, it fails as a numbers game, same goes with referrals, the more people you ask for referrals, the more times they're going to say, oh yeah, it's hit or miss, you get it, sometimes they're not going to do it, but most of the time they will, and every once in a while, you get one of those, I said it last time, Farron TAC, it was my company that referred me like 200 mortgages, it's like I made $1 million, well for one referer, because I knocked on their fucking door and cold called them, and they loved me, I cold called them, I closed them, I got into ultra tight rapport, I had moved them logically and emotionally, and they felt comfortable to start referring me, right on the spot, that very day I got three referrals for the first thing, then their people, I made sure by the way, there's another thing, is that once you get to the referral and you close the referer, the referral, make sure, say by the way, if you give your call back to the person who referred you, let Jim know, let John know, how good a John, I really appreciate it, so they then called, wow thanks, that guy was great, and you get more referrals from them, and also once you've done that, you now set them up, the new person to give you referrals as well, you're setting up a referral chain, remember I always call that creating a never ending stream of customer referrals, I teach a seven step process for generating a massive steady stream, a never ending stream of referrals, now if this was one of those bulls, webbids that say, and for $2,000, you have to go buy my product out, I'm going to give it to you on Saturday, because we have time on the intensive, so you can learn it this week, everyone who's doing it is also a member of the intensive, so you get that, it's part of the deal, so again, today is your daily motivation, I want you to be out there, every door, every phone call, every closed sale, I want you to ask your referrals, and ask in the right way and expect them to give you referrals, don't wait, you don't have to wait any arbitrary period of time, essentially what we're doing here, we're building a fully integrated sales machine for you, new blood, referred blood, and also we're going to start talking about how you develop bigger and bigger orders of people as well, I love you all, go out there today and kick ass, remember, every closed sale represents the potential for five more sales, but you have to ask, don't wait, don't feel shy or embarrassed or feel like you're putting someone off or you're doing something that's not right, this is the time to ask, right when you're on the phone for that first time, they feel certain bam, ask your referrals and I promise you the results will be staggering, I love you all, speak again tomorrow, take care.
